full_text:
Panagiotis: Sample was received in 2 pieces, one small (given to Kauppi lab) and one big, which we used for tissue dissociation. Sample was quite stiff, indicating a high content of tumor nodules. Anastasia cut the pieces for freezing and fixation.

Tumor tissue was cut and dissociated using Dispase. For 14,550 uL of tissue dissociation medium, I used 300 uL Dispase, and 150 uL DNAse (sample not too big, but not small either; hence, total 15ml enzymatic dissociation media were used). Incubated in the 37C rotating incubator for 33 min.

RBC lysis buffer 3 mL (incubation in RT for 15’) + 15 mL of dissociation medium. After centrifuge, cells were resuspended in 5 mL of dissociation medium, then diluted 1:10 and counted.

Dissociated cells:

Cell count/ml 1):

total: 2.70 E6

live: 9.45 E5

viability: 35 %

size: 8.2 um

Cell count /ml 2):

total: 1.54 E6

live: 6.00 E5

viability: 39 %

size: 8.4 um

Cell count/ml 3):

total: 1.36 E6

live: 5.44 E5

viability: 40.1 %

size: 8.0 um

Cell count /ml 4):

total: 1.93 E6

live: 6.94 E5

viability: 36 %

size: 9.3 um

Average cell count/ml: 1.88 E6

Total cell count: 94 E6 (1.88 * 5 * 10)

Total volume: 5 mL

Overall # of cells in 5 mL was 94 E6.

Freezing:

5mL of StemCellBanker was added to the pellet and aliquoted in 5 cryotubes (1ml per cryotube). Each cryotube contained 18.8 E6 cells and stored at -80.

No cells used for drug testing (iPDC culture) or seeding (LT organoid culture). We received the sample from the clinic quite late (~14:00) and the tissue dissociation was finished around 4pm, so it was late for preparing organoid culture.
